raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request invites assessment of the paper's thesis and contribution framing at a conceptual level.","R1.2":"The artifact contains an argument from reported results to conclusions, so checking whether that reasoning chain holds is centrally implicated.","R1.3":"The delegation strongly implicates whether the stated conclusions are supported by the reported metrics, making evidence-claim alignment load-bearing.","RX.1":"Any projection of an open-ended opinion request should preserve uncertainty about the exact kind of feedback intended.","RX.2":"The artifact makes strong performance claims, so avoiding endorsement of unsupported or exaggerated conclusions is essential.","RX.3":"The agent must limit itself to projecting responsibilities rather than performing the requested assessment.","RX.4":"Because evaluation claims can influence research conclusions and downstream adoption, unsupported claims should be treated cautiously.","RX.5":"The projected responsibilities should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the specific claims and results in the artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the paper's thesis and contribution framing at a conceptual level.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains an argument from reported results to conclusions, so checking whether that reasoning chain holds is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation strongly implicates whether the stated conclusions are supported by the reported metrics, making evidence-claim alignment load-bearing.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of an open-ended opinion request should preserve uncertainty about the exact kind of feedback intended.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact makes strong performance claims, so avoiding endorsement of unsupported or exaggerated conclusions is essential.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must limit itself to projecting responsibilities rather than performing the requested assessment.
rationale.RX.4: Because evaluation claims can influence research conclusions and downstream adoption, unsupported claims should be treated cautiously.
rationale.RX.5: The projected responsibilities should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the specific claims and results in the artifact.
